why: Issue #43: every KIPRIS fixture was hand-authored, so the adapter suite certified the bug it was meant to catch (#38, #39). This manifest makes fixture origin an enforced, reviewable fact instead of a test docstring.

Origin is not enough on its own. A RECORDED happy-path body satisfies 'one real response per capability' while certifying nothing — #39 was itself a happy-path-shaped bug. So network capabilities must also cover the edge shapes listed in required_shapes, and each shape is proven by REPLAYING the bytes through the adapter, never by trusting the label below.

origins.recorded: Byte-for-byte capture of a real service response via scripts/record_fixtures.py, credential-scrubbed with volatile fields pinned. The only origin that can prove a live contract.
origins.derived: Produced by mutating recorded bytes to reach a shape the live service would not return on demand (an error envelope, a truncated body). Honest about not being a real observation.
origins.synthetic: Authored by hand. Legitimate ONLY where no live service is involved (scoring goldens). Never acceptable as the oracle for a network adapter's success path.
origins.unverified: Pre-dates this manifest and its origin was never established. Treat as synthetic; retire or re-record.

offline_adapters.manual_web: Makes no network call — normalizes user-supplied rows. Can never have a recorded response, so it is excluded from capability coverage by construction rather than by omission.

fixtures.note: The response that exposed #38: <count> is a SIBLING of <body>, never nested inside it. Carries pipe-delimited ipcNumber (#39) and two distinct registerStatus values (소멸, 등록). Both records are load-bearing for status_variant.
Zero-hit search. Proves <items/> self-closing with totalCount 0 parses as an empty success, not as a failure.

First real response this capability has ever produced — it had zero production callers until --capability was added. Confirms #41 with evidence: the SAME patent as word-search-live-v1.xml (1020160062884) is returned as 10-2016-0062884 with applicationDate 2016.05.23, versus 20160523 from word_search. Same source_locator, different content_hash.

Carries patent_link, serpapi_link and serpapi_pagination — all three read by the adapter and all three absent from the invented organic-results-v1.json. filing_date arrives as 2020-11-20, a third date format alongside KIPRIS's two.
Page 2, with serpapi_pagination.previous and .next. The has_more/next_cursor branch had zero positive coverage before this.
Hand-authored error envelope. Satisfies the error shape only because provoking a real 429 costs quota and is not reliably reproducible; re-record opportunistically if one is ever observed.
#43: this file and the marker list it validates were written in the same commit from the same guess, so it cannot corroborate the parser.

Invented: born in 455eb1c in the same diff as the adapter, before any live call existed. Omits patent_link, serpapi_link and serpapi_pagination. Superseded as an oracle by organic-results-live-v1.json; retained only while call sites are migrated.

Minimal single-record fixture for adapter unit tests (pagination, redirect guard, canary scrub). REBUILT into the real sibling-<count> shape — it no longer certifies the pre-#38 nested-<body> shape that let #38 ship. Synthetic content in the real structure, so 'derived'. Not a coverage oracle: word-search-live-v1.xml is the recorded happy_path.
Quota preflight body, not a search response. #44 verified the live account shape matches, but committed nothing.
Exhausted-quota preflight body. Deliberately not reproduced live: exhausting the plan to record it would cost the whole monthly allowance.
Scoring golden. Synthetic BY DESIGN — it pins deterministic arithmetic, involves no service, and there is nothing to record.

xfail_allowlist.reason: The error shape is covered by a hand-authored envelope (error-rate-limit.json), not a recorded 429. Provoking a real rate limit costs quota and is not reliably reproducible.
